frameworks.name: cognitive map
frameworks.one_line: map-like neural representation of allocentric space in hippocampus and cortex

frameworks.name: neural state-space geometry
frameworks.one_line: analysing how task variables structure fMRI representational geometry

frameworks.name: joint current-and-prospective place code
frameworks.one_line: computational model jointly encoding present and goal locations

frameworks.name: goal-directed navigation
frameworks.one_line: flexible behaviour as the source of representational distortion

core_question: How does goal-directed behaviour distort the neural representation of allocentric space?
key_assumptions: allocentric space is represented as a cognitive map in hippocampus and neocortex
fMRI representational geometry reflects how task variables are encoded
contextual cues can make one goal conditionally dependent on another
manipulations: successive goal locations in a four-room grid world
contextual cue setting conditional dependence between goals
room identity versus context as encoded task variables
key_findings: map-like representations of the environment emerged in hippocampus and neocortex
cognitive maps in hippocampus and orbitofrontal cortex were compressed so goal-cued locations clustered in neural state space
the degree of compression predicted successful learning
interpretations: goal-seeking compresses spatial codes by jointly encoding current and prospective locations
this distortion of the cognitive map supports flexible goal-directed behaviour
